topic: Thinking of starting a business in Oman? Here is the first document the bank will ask you for
format: List the 9 documents a complete feasibility study includes — and what each one tells the bank
post:
The bank didn't reject his idea. They rejected his paperwork.

A friend in Muscat spent 4 months getting ready to apply for DBO funding. Good concept, real demand, solid numbers in his head. The bank asked for a feasibility study. He handed them a 3-page Word document.

Application stalled.

So what does a complete feasibility study actually look like — and why does each section matter to the person reviewing it?

1. Executive Summary — your whole business on one page
2. Project Description — what you're building and why it works
3. Market Analysis — proof there's a real customer waiting
4. Technical Study — operations, location, equipment, staffing
5. Organisational Structure — who runs what, and how
6. Financial Projections — 3–5 year P&L, cash flow, break-even
7. Funding Requirements — how much, from where, on what terms
8. Risk Assessment — shows you've thought about what could go wrong
9. Appendices — licenses, CVs, supporting data

The bank isn't betting on your passion. It's betting on your preparation.

Most first-time applicants skip sections 7 and 8 entirely. Those are the first places reviewers look.

Build a bank-ready feasibility study free in ~10 minutes — covers all 10 Development Bank Oman / Riyada programs: https://www.thesmartpro.io/feasibility-studio?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=feasibility
